name: Gibraltar Stele Loop
description: This difficult 7.8-mile (12.5 km) jogging loop near Saint-Palais offers a challenging workout with 1117 feet (340 metres) of elevation gain.
region_intro: Jogging around Saint-Palais offers diverse landscapes, blending coastal paths with forested areas and rolling hills. The region features extensive coastal trails, sandy beaches, and natural rock formations, providing varied terrain for runners. Pine forests and dunes also contribute to the natural environment, offering shaded routes. The area's topography includes moderate elevation changes, suitable for different fitness levels. Best jogging routes around Saint-Palais The most popular jogging route is Loop of Saint-Palais, a 7.8 miles (12.5 km) trail that takes 1 hour 31 minutes to complete. This route offers a challenging experience with significant elevation gain. Another top favourite among local runners is Gibraltar Stele – Chapelle de Soyarza loop from Amendeuix-Oneix, a difficult 12.5 miles (20.1 km) path. This route traverses varied terrain, including hills and open landscapes. Local runners also love the Tranchée d'Arbouet loop from Aïcirits, a 6.7 miles (10.7 km) trail leading through rural landscapes, often completed in about 1 hour 12 minutes. Jogging around Saint-Palais is defined by coastal paths, beaches, and forested areas. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult ability levels. The routes in Saint-Palais are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.1 stars from over 8 reviews. More than 370 runners have used komoot to explore Saint-Palais's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Saint-Palais?
A: Saint-Palais offers a diverse network of over 25 running routes. These trails cater to various fitness levels, with options ranging from easy coastal paths to more challenging routes through varied terrain.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on jogging routes in Saint-Palais?
A: Jogging in Saint-Palais provides a mix of landscapes. You'll find extensive coastal paths like the &lt;em&gt;Sentier des Douaniers&lt;/em&gt;, sandy beaches such as Plage du Bureau and La Grande Côte, and shaded runs through pine forests like &lt;em&gt;Le Platin Forest&lt;/em&gt;. Some routes also feature moderate elevation changes and rolling hills.

Q: Are there easy jogging routes for beginners in Saint-Palais?
A: Yes, Saint-Palais has 5 easy jogging routes, perfect for beginners or those looking for a relaxed run. These often include flat coastal sections or gentle paths through forested areas, providing a pleasant experience without significant elevation.

Q: Can I find challenging running trails with significant elevation gain?
A: Absolutely. For experienced runners seeking a challenge, Saint-Palais offers 5 difficult routes. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e879745587/loop-of-saint-palais" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Loop of Saint-Palais&lt;/a&gt; is a 7.8-mile (12.5 km) trail with over 340 meters of elevation gain. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20161503" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gibraltar Stele – Chapelle de Soyarza loop from Amendeuix-Oneix&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 12.5 miles (20.1 km) with nearly 600 meters of ascent.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see while jogging in Saint-Palais?
A: Many running routes in Saint-Palais offer scenic views and pass by notable landmarks. Along the coastal paths, you can admire the striking &lt;em&gt;Pont du Diable&lt;/em&gt; (Devil's Bridge) rock formation and the natural area of &lt;em&gt;Les Pierrières&lt;/em&gt;. Further inland, routes might take you past historical sites like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5147854" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gibraltar Stele&lt;/a&gt; or the charming &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/930773" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chapelle de Soyarza&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in Saint-Palais?
A: Yes, many of the running routes in Saint-Palais are circular, offering varied scenery without retracing your steps. Popular options include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e879745587/loop-of-saint-palais" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Loop of Saint-Palais&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40034197" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tranchée d'Arbouet loop from Aïcirits&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you through rural landscapes.

Q: What do other runners say about the jogging routes in Saint-Palais?
A: The running routes in Saint-Palais are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.1 stars from over 8 reviews. More than 370 runners have explored the area, often praising the diverse terrain, from coastal views to peaceful forest trails.

Q: Is parking available near the running routes in Saint-Palais?
A: Given Saint-Palais's popularity as an outdoor destination, parking is generally available near trailheads and popular beaches. Many coastal access points and town centers offer parking facilities, though it's always advisable to check specific starting points for convenience.

Q: What is the best time of year for running in Saint-Palais?
A: Saint-Palais, particularly Saint-Palais-sur-Mer, enjoys a pleasant seaside climate. Spring and autumn offer mild temperatures ideal for running, with fewer crowds. Summer is also popular, but coastal breezes can make even warmer days comfortable. Winter runs can be refreshing, especially along the less crowded coastal paths.

Q: Are the jogging trails in Saint-Palais suitable for families?
A: Yes, many of the easier and moderate routes, especially those along the beaches or through the flatter sections of &lt;em&gt;Le Platin Forest&lt;/em&gt;, are suitable for families. These paths often provide a safe and enjoyable environment for all ages to run or walk together.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the running trails in Saint-Palais?
A: Many outdoor areas in Saint-Palais are dog-friendly, particularly natural paths and forest trails. However, regulations can vary, especially on beaches during peak seasons. It's always best to keep your dog on a leash and check local signage for specific rules regarding pets on trails and in protected natural areas.

Q: Are there places to eat or drink near the jogging routes?
A: Saint-Palais-sur-Mer, being a coastal town, has numerous cafes, restaurants, and shops, especially around the town center and near popular beaches like Plage du Bureau. You'll find plenty of options to refuel or relax after your run, often with scenic views.
tips: In the former Franciscan monastery there is now a modern tourist and pilgrim information center which also rents pilgrim rooms in the old monastery.
Near Ostabat, the Gibraltar Stele marks the spot where the three great routes to Santiago meet.

Saint-Palais was the capital of the Kingdom of Navarre until 1521, as evidenced by its rich cultural heritage. In the heart of the Pyrenees, this town is the starting point for the Great Crossing of the Basque Country by mountain bike.

faqs.question: How long does it take to complete the Loop of Saint-Palais?
faqs.answer: The Loop of Saint-Palais is a challenging jogging route that typically takes around 1 hour and 31 minutes to complete. This estimate is for experienced runners due to the trail's difficulty and elevation gain.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this jogging route?
faqs.answer: This route is rated as 'difficult' and a 'Hard run' on Komoot. It requires very good fitness and some sure-footedness due to its varied terrain and significant elevation gain. It's best suited for experienced joggers looking for a rigorous workout.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Loop of Saint-Palais?
faqs.answer: The terrain is quite varied, contributing to its challenging nature. You'll encounter a mix of asphalt, paved surfaces, and unpaved sections. The route also includes dedicated paths, roads, streets, and footpaths, offering a diverse running environment.

faqs.question: Are there any notable landmarks or points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route passes by a few points of interest. You'll find the Chemins-Bideak Pilgrims' and Tourist Information Center in Saint-Palais, which can be a useful stop. Another notable feature is the Gibraltar Stele, a significant man-made monument.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Loop of Saint-Palais?
faqs.answer: There is a parking lot available at the starting point of the Loop of Saint-Palais, making it accessible for those traveling by car. For more detailed information on parking or public transport options, you might check local tourism resources like tourisme64.com.

faqs.question: Is the Loop of Saint-Palais dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While specific restrictions for dogs on this particular route are not detailed, generally, trails in the Pyrénées-Atlantiques region are often dog-friendly. However, it's always recommended to keep dogs on a leash, especially given the varied terrain and potential for encountering wildlife or other trail users. Please check local signage for any specific rules.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to jog this route?
faqs.answer: The Pyrénées-Atlantiques region generally offers pleasant conditions for outdoor activities during spring and autumn. These seasons typically avoid the peak heat of summer and the colder, potentially wetter conditions of winter, making them ideal for a challenging run like this. Always check the local weather forecast before heading out.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay an entrance fee to use the trail?
faqs.answer: Based on available information, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to jog the Loop of Saint-Palais. It is generally accessible to the public. However, it's always a good idea to consult local tourism information, such as tourisme64.com, for any last-minute updates or specific regulations.

faqs.question: Does the Loop of Saint-Palais intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route intersects with several official trails. You'll find sections that overlap with the Grande Traversée du Pays Basque Étape 1, Orthez - Estérençuby, and the Chemin de Xibaltarre / Xibaltarreko bidea. It also crosses parts of the EV3 France 49, Saint-Palais - Saint-Jean-Pied-de-Port, and Le Chemin de Saint-Jacques, among others.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect along the route?
faqs.answer: While specific scenic details for this exact inland loop are not extensively documented, the broader Saint-Palais area in the Pyrénées-Atlantiques is known for its position at the crossroads of the Basque Country. You can expect to traverse varied landscapes, including valleys and hamlets, and potentially catch views of the Basque mountains. The region often features cultural and historical elements like steles.

faqs.question: Is the Loop of Saint-Palais suitable for beginner joggers?
faqs.answer: No, the Loop of Saint-Palais is not recommended for beginner joggers. It is rated as a 'difficult' and 'Hard run' due to its 12.5 km length, 340 meters of elevation gain, and varied terrain. It requires very good fitness and some sure-footedness, making it more suitable for experienced runners.

waypoints.name: Chemins-Bideak Pilgrims' and Tourist Information Center, Saint-Palais
